commands.review_summary:
The PR lowers the adaptive `read` cap for small model context windows, preserves explicit limits, adds byte-budgeted continuation notices, and expands read-tool regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +137, Tests +184. Total +321 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than a live model: current main clamps adaptive read sizing to at least 32KB, while the PR tests the 16k-token case at an 8KB cap. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer judgment on the compatibility-sensitive smaller implicit read default.

target.title: fix(agents): scale read output for small contexts
target.body:
This keeps default `read` output smaller for small-context local models without changing explicit caller limits. It is part of the small-model stability work: smaller models were getting oversized file reads by default, then losing useful context or stalling around follow-up tool use.

Refs #86599.

## Summary

- Scale the adaptive default `read` byte cap from the active model context window, with an 8KB floor and 128KB ceiling.
- Preserve explicit `limit` values, including `0` and negative values that the base read tool normalizes to one line.
- Keep continuation guidance inside the byte budget and avoid misleading offsets when the first line or trailing whitespace-only lines hit the cap.
- Add focused coverage for small-context caps, long lines, explicit limits, whitespace-only capped content, EOF pagination, and continuation-text-in-file false positives.

## Verification

Latest pushed head: `8b5f3c3b90e` on base `67ddc1a3e13`.

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed after rebasing onto `67ddc1a3e13`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed on pushed SHA `8b5f3c3b90e`: 1 routed Vitest shard, 15 tests, 4.39s.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with exit 0 on SHA `e7b5f8011e4` rebased on `a17487bc9f6`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_febf18c48862`, run `run_71149f3d0e40`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, lease stopped.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` also passed with exit 0: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_b583a46f44af`, run `run_1c3eeae37d42`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` previously passed clean: no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: small-context models get a smaller default file-read payload, while explicit caller limits keep the existing read contract.

Real environment tested: local focused node-wrapper tests for read-tool behavior; AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

Evidence after fix: focused read-tool tests passed on pushed SHA `8b5f3c3b90e`; AWS Crabbox `run_71149f3d0e40` completed with exit 0 and covered the `core`/`coreTests` lanes.

Observed result after fix: adaptive reads cap output for small context windows, explicit limits still delegate to the base read tool, capped whitespace-only pages keep emitted content aligned with continuation offsets, and continuation-looking file text is not treated as pagination metadata.

What was not tested: real Ollama/model-provider live chat; this PR only changes read-tool context sizing, not provider streaming. `origin/main` moved after the remote gate, so the final pushed SHA has fresh focused test/diff proof plus the prior same-patch Crabbox gate.
